title: Understanding the Problem
text: To find the maximum force of friction, we need to use the formula for static friction: \( F_{friction} $$=$$ \mu \times F_{normal} \). Here, \( \mu \) is the coefficient of static friction, and \( F_{normal} \) is the normal force.

title: Identifying the Normal Force
text: In this scenario, the normal force \( F_{normal} \) is the force exerted on the joint, which is $$10$$ times the weight being supported. If the weight is \( W \), then \( F_{normal} $$=$$ $$10$$ \times W \).

title: Calculating the Maximum Force of Friction
text: Using the formula \( F_{friction} $$=$$ \mu \times F_{normal} \), substitute \( \mu $$=$$ $$0.016$$ \) and \( F_{normal} $$=$$ $$10$$ \times W \).

title: Final Calculation
text: Assuming the weight \( W \) is $$64.5$$ N, calculate the maximum force of friction.
